channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 78 replies — 170.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-05-19 05:18 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62238370
>>62238083
any sort of model.predict() is going to overfit unless you train on like 20-30% of the data thats implicitly going to be trained or predicted on, so with pure ta or price action strategies overfitting is never possible since theyre raw calculations of mathematics on price action time series data


holy shit anon you should know this im sharing liquid gold intuition with you

62238387
>>62219625
That equity curve is either a data issue or clearly just picking pennies up infront of a steamroller.
You being obssessed with "smoothness" is also a red flag.
You thinking "the data backs it up" with confidence is also a BIG red flag, this is not how real trading works.
Anyone can just run some lookahead biased regressions and uncover a "strategy", anything that is actually statistically robust and ACTIONABLE will be based off of forward walks and have effect sizes so small you'll be questioning whether its even real, and only THEN have you found something that might actually indeed be real.

62238401
>>62238370
I tried a variety of strategies based on price action but I could never get any of them to work. This setup is the best I could get. I tried other things but they wouldn’t work. I’m still not understanding how this ML would filter out losses in a way that’s actionable. If it just gives me some highly specific bucket like “trades between 0.75% and 0.84% of OR width are losing” then that’s just over fitting.

>>62238387
I’ve mentioned why. It’s for the sake of prop firms. They have hard drawdown rules. As mentioned before, i can theoretically pass and even get on a funded account with the strat as it is now. But it takes too long to pass and too long to get a payout. Most of what I read in ur response sounds like fluff no offense. I’m all for constructive criticism but I don’t see how any of what you said applies to my.

My strategy is a blend of 5 simple ideas that each trade on their own with the idea of:

Rather than find one single strategy that is good and trades often,
Find multiple good strategies that don’t trade often individually and then use them together.

I haven’t done any look back biased regressions, I have a separate 2026 only sample that I’ll test my strategies on to make sure they’re not overfit. And they’ll work just fine on new never before seen data.

Guys I am open to any constructive criticism, just please make it make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238500
>>62238401
Listen bro you don't just throw a couple of funny numbers in a computer and cash comes out thats just not how the world works.
You believe you need to be super opaque so we don't "steal" your strategy, but there are thousands of guys like you who think they have some top secret "strat" that they just need someone to fund them for.
What model are you even using? Some GARCH? HMM? Frequency of your data?

62238660
> top secret strategy trades the Nasdaq, an index that only goes up
> it makes money
> anon thinks he's a genius
> loses to buy-and-hold TQQQ

62238773
>>62238500
I’m not being opaque. I said I want advice from someone who has knowledge in fields related to statistics and quantitative analysis. Do you?

No, I don’t use GARCH. My strategy does not tune itself, it does not use some regressive or adjusting method. It follows the same rules trading in 2021 as it does in 2025 in the data set. The TP and SL’s are the only dynamic variable because they are based on VWAP, or ORB, or the volume profile as well as fixed % based on the open market value. A 20 point move in 2020 is far different than a 20 point move in 2025.
But they are not hand tuned or adjusted by me. It sets the TP based on the given range. It’s as simple as that.

I only used volume and OHLC of NQ and ES via 1m candles.

Also who the hell said I need funding? You should read all of my messages before making assumptions.

>>62238660
The worst drawdowns were approximately 3,000 points, while peak equity was around 50,000 points.

This is just napkin math, but let’s make a few simplifying assumptions:

Assume 1 NQ contract per trade. Some lines had multiple take-profits, but we’ll simplify. NQ is $20 per point, so a 3,000-point drawdown would require $60,000 in capital to withstand the worst observed drawdown. To be more conservative, let’s round that up to $100,000 of starting cash. Unexessary but let’s do it anyways.

Starting with $100,000, if running equity reaches 50,000 points, that’s 50,000 × $20 = $1,000,000.

So, theoretically, starting with $100,000 and ending around $1 million is possible under these assumptions.

TQQQ went up about 230% from 2020 till 2025, where my data ends.

230% vs 900%.

If I had the money, I’d just put it all into SCHD and chill. But I don’t. That’s why I made this thread. That’s why I’m making this strategy and asking if anyone here can offer ideas that make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238820
>>62238773
duh you don't hold TQQQ through 2022 retard
I already understand you don't want to apply any brains but jesus

62239204
>>62238500
lmfao, garch, mm, and HMM are all useless in trading

62239213
>>62238773
try this just for the lols and its like throwing shit to a wall hoping it sticks

code the strategy using an external data api use every talib indicator an oscilator then denoise the indicators then shove that as a filter and vectorize the backtest extract the highest sharpe ratio or net return in similar sharpe give or take in your datamining script

62239220
>>62239213
if you dont know anything about genetic algorithms watch coding trains NEAT flappy bird series its quite useful information and intuition for genetic algorithms doesnt have to be neuroevolution since these are just indicators you wont be topologically ranking these to another sorting neuron layer you just need to mutate and generate fitness effectively

62239225
if all of this is above your head just fucking quit quant isnt cut out for noobs its cut out for mathematics genius' and physicists

62239227
also i gave your ass free alpha i expect you to post source code now

62240221
>>62239213
I already tried something similar to this. I didn’t cover the entire library, but I used a more pointed approach that was computationally feasible for me and something I could actually verify myself.

The issue is that a lot of strategies perform all right, but they’re not smooth enough for a prop firm. What I’m showing is not the only thing I found, but it’s the smoothest thing I’ve found so far.

The others either weren’t as smooth or had large drawdown spikes.

Also there's no such thing as a single indicator that will perform well. If I do find sometrhing, it's going to be a combination of some, so I'd have to test a lot of permutations. Walking forward through millions of candles, testing hundreds if not thousands of combos, and then multiplying that again to find the optimal TP / SL setup. that is going to take a lot of computation and a hell of a lot of time of waiting. Not a smart approach.

Besides, I don't need a new strategy. I already have one. I just need to optimize it without suffering from overfitting. The only way I could do that is to have some new static filter. One thing I was looking at was introducing volumetrics and using that to filter bad trades, and it actually did help.

What I might do is try to combine the library with what I currently have and see if it improves in some way. Though that does open a whole can of worms. We'll see.


>>62239220
Flappy Bird is a clean fixed environment. trading ain't.

62240722
>>62240221
Nvm you can’t be helped you’re just trying to act smart at this point lmfao

62240724
bump once more before I wake up

62240823
>>62240221
do you even know why i recommended you to use a genetic algorithm? or you just blab shit like flappy bird if a fixed env? its about the intuition for GA's lol you are stupid as fuck nigga

62240835
>>62224277
Have you tried filtering on volatility anon? It tends to help trend following system. It could be something as simple as an inside bar chart pattern.

62240836
>>62240835
Don’t help him he’s retarded

62240887
>>62240835
I actually did try that. I tried filtering over a few fixed periods. However, all the trades that would be cut out would also include winners in an equal ratio. I couldn't really get any sort of volatility filter to cut out only losers.

I've been a bit hesitant to implement a filter that's based off of candlestick patterns, just because I think that's the weakest form of analysis. Analysis based on volumetrics or analysis based on trends or volatility, etc., that makes more sense to me compared to just analysis based on “price is forming an inverted cup or an upside-down H”

That's been my results from a lot of filters. All the filters will cut out trades, but they'll cut out an equal amount of wins and losses. Or if they do cut out more losses, the ratio is not really that much. I am still sure that there is some method that I could use, because I'm averaging eight trades a day. In my mind, surely there has to be a way to cut that down to just two and drastically increase win rate.

62241679
i understand about 2% of what you guys are bickering about, but here's a ChatGPT QRD of the thread cause it's funny

62242217
>>62241679
lol then dont comment im a pro

62242682
>>62241679
I've been asking the same question but people don't seem to understand it.
I already have a working strategy. I want to improve it. I've explained the avenue I want to go down to improve it, and I've asked if that's even possible.

90% of the responses have been irrelevant, like telling me to go in a vague ML direction or telling me to start from scratch looking at new strategies.

62242797
>>62242682
orb is shit nigga lmao

62243824
>>62242682
win rate is not as important as the type of risk to return you have. you want to make sure you can find and take as many trades as possible that fits your specific strategy. you want to win ideally more then 3x your risk so no matter what you dont go to zero. the longer you play the game without going bust the better your odds are of becoming the 1% that become a profitable trader long term. and i dont mean purely expected value tables you need to understand your own set up to know if a stop limit if best or if youre pricing things to zero etc.

62243848
>>62243824
Winrate, Profit Factor, R2 ratio, and ideally 1-2 trades a day. That's what I measure my strategies by. But PF and R2 are the more important ones.

I tried to implement a 3:1 and 2:1RR strategy but it's performance metrics were not good enough, specifically PF.

>the better your odds are of becoming the 1% that become a profitable trader long term
the strategy IS profitable long term. It's just not suited for prop firm rules.

62243869
>>62243848
regardless of how you slice it your returns need to be larger then. i would look back at my losing trades that had the same setup and try to find another way to look what different about it vs the ones that were successful, also try to do the same thing on inverse products or something with an obvious downtrend and see if you can find out a new way to rule out the less likely versions of your setup. for instance youd likely get less opporutnities in a bear trend and can see why that specific time worked there something like that might benefit you

62243906
>>62243869
>see if you can find out a new way to rule out the less likely versions of your setup
That's what im looking for ATM, i don't need more trades I need less trades of higher quality. But doing that is easier said than done lol.

I am currently looking into some higher time frame analysis, trying to see if I can change how the strategy works based on regime, and maybe some time specific stuff; trades going into lunch time are less likely to win etc. But I don't think these will take my strategy from 54% to 65% plus, i need something more but im not sure.

62247318
bump

62248009
>>62243848
lmfao

62248300
I’ll bump a few more times before letting the thread die. Seems like I couldn’t get any help from here

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 104 replies — 144.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-05-19 05:03 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109
>>62234021
do the alternative. Privacy

62234248
>>62234021
Government is shit.

62234899
>>62233986
I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090
>>62220146
give me money

62237423
>>62219995
you're rich mate.

62238430
>>62229077
Exactly, but turning BTC into an active asset will through native staking, instead of just worrying about future risks.

62238954
>>62234248
I'm not surprised lol

62239105
>just buy into the obvious ponzi scheme bro

62240786
>>62228616
hmm find better opportunities

62241339
>>62219942
Soon I will get to that 1 BTC mark, RN I'm buying more and staking some.

62244007
>>62238430
That's the right move anon

62244072
>>62219942
Bitcoin is getting fucked by the Jews

62246429
>>62241339
I know you can do it anon

62246430
>>62239105
If it has a high likelihood to make me rich then I dont care

62246437
>>62244072
it's just the cycle

62246447
>>62219942
I don't want to buy btc

62246571
>>62221733
>Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future
It still blows my mind that people thought rare Pepes could actually be a real thing.

62248268
>>62244072
Including you?

62248275
>>62222594
Bad actors are not going to have access to a quantum computer before things get figured out.

item.title: KEK MICROSOFT — 30 replies — 120.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
KEK MICROSOFT

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-19 05:33 UTC

OP

KEK MICROSOFT

Early replies (first 3)

62242596
>>62242594
Fake news. Doesn't he own Microsoft? As far as I know that means you can't sell the stock since it's literally your company.

62242597
Just in time before a windows defender exploit

62242598
>>62242594
Micropenis lel, didnt read btw.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62242604
>>62242594
>3bn

who cares? it has like a 3T market cap, 3bn is literally nothing and doesnt matter

62242615
>>62242596
>Doesn't he own Microsoft?
No

62242641
>>62242594
Good riddance.
Ever since bill gaytes started stock dumping, it has been pumping.
Steve Ballmer held all his stock and he's now far richer than Billy boy.

62242765
>>62242596
It's a public company you tard

62242784
>>62242596
Nope. It's real.

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/bill-gates-just-did-unthinkable-132955202.html

>The Bill Gates Foundation Trust exited its final position by selling all remaining shares after reducing holdings from 28.5M to zero shares, a move driven by portfolio diversification, valuation optimization, and funding obligations rather than confidence loss.

>Gates’ complete exit from Microsoft reflects rational asset allocation for a charitable foundation managing liquidity needs and concentration risk, not a loss of faith in Microsoft’s AI-driven business transformation and ability to convert enterprise technology investments into recurring revenue.

Methinks they doth protest too much.

62242804
1. Why did he do that?
2. What does it mean for the company's future and its stocks?

62242984
>jeet CEO
the new IBM
zero

62242991
>>62242804
I heard co-pilot licenses are about to go up like 15x the price. Maybe the AI bubble is about to pop.

62243539
>>62242594
UH MICROJEET BROS??

62243590
>>62242596
Holy shit how can people so dumb come and post on a finance board. Truly shows why this place sucks.

62243606
>>62243590
>newfag can’t decipher obvious b8 and has a Redditor meltdown

62243628
>>62243606
No it's not bait, this board is pretty low. I have 16 filtered threads right now, people are really low IQ here. Shame.

62243736
>>62243590
this board was better in 2018 when alt seasoned happened

62243990
>>62242604
Market cap doesn’t = liquidity. All that matters is liquidity. It’s why some shit coin can be worth 100 million but if liquidity is low some dude could sell 1 million dollars and price goes down 30% reducing market cap by 30 million with a 1 million sell order

62244084
>>62243628
16kek, i have 34, prolly only lankshit.

62244361
>>62242991
bullshit

the prices of m365 copilot licences are decreasing since 2023

t. copilot security engineer

62244369
>>62242596
Yes, he owns 100% shares, writes 100% of the code and if you call the IT support, the guy with the indian accent is actually him.

62245917
>>62244361
Not sure if trolling or intentional misinformation.

62246079
>>62243590
>Holy shit
back you go

62246475
What's he mainly own now then?

62246675
>>62242594
He made a deal related to the epstein shit. I just know it. He's being forced to do this, he has to be.

62246689
>>62243539
Nooooo bill gates do not redeem the stock

62246908
>>62246675
I guess, yea, but the foundation has his ex-wife's name in it so he probably just shutting it down rather than renaming it. I don't think he can rename it with her permission and she probably wants to get paid for when it shuts down. She is likely forcing him to liquidate it so she can get her cut. Remember, they opened it together when they first married so she is entitled to half.

62247039
>>62243736
>shitcoins
yeah no, i rode the wave too but the amount of people believing in [insert shitty internet dogshitcoin] being the new paradigm was way too many, smart people boughted and sold asap.
then theres link baggots.
im glad stocks are a lot more prevalent now desu

62247098
>>62243990
>checks MSFT
>up
nope

62247562
>>62242641
>Steve Ballmer held all his stock
cocaine rendered him physically unable to be bearish

62248326
>>62242604
>bill gates' confidence in microsoft is sufficiently low that he considers it prudent to sell his ENTIRE position
i'm sure that means nothing

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 13 replies — 17.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-19 05:01 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62246449
XMR will offer better returns

62246464
>>62245741
>If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?
Bitcoin will be banned because of Iran

62246468
>hes shilling goy gold in May of 2026
Grim

62246506
>>62245757
This, I ain’t fuckin buying pumped bags.

62246878
>>62245760
Self-custody really becomes important once people understand why Bitcoin was created in the first place.

62247229
>>62246449
When new ATH?

62247257
All in on BTC and BOTZ

62247324
>>62245741
Nty. I'd rather buy s&p and get a 10x hahah.

62247434
>>62245741
I can't buy much. First my other crypto which have performed well since buying them reach their target prices and then maybe I rotate into BTC.

62248271
>>62246464
That's not possible lol.

item.title: I've lost 40 years worth of investing and hard work so... — 21 replies — 15.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've lost 40 years worth of investing and hard work so...

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-19 04:26 UTC

OP

...so let me cope before I kill myself.
ITT we discuss povertism and how money is useless and not important.

Early replies (first 3)

62243479
Buddhist monks own nothing and are unironically happier than billionaires

62243636
>>62243479
There should be a way of reaching one's goals other than through money; also, not everyone with money reaches his goals.
Fuck my life!

62243648
>>62243452
Did u buy 1dte calls on Friday

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62243722
>>62243479
donations, communal support, temple assets, and very low personal expenses.

they are glorified beggars under the guise of cleanliness, discipline and seeking enlightentment. You could live with next to no money if you lived the way they do. They live by spending no monney becasue the holes are plugged up by the aforementioned donations and support. but in reality u do not want that life cuz.

>>62243636
Depends on the goals, but yeah i'd say 90% of goals require money. And the goals that don't require money require time and attention that most ppl cant afford to put into it cuz they dont have enough money lmao.

62243783
>>62243452
howd you do that OP
help us learn from your example

62243804
>>62243722
fuck off, why do you want to kill me?

62243807
>>62243722
They actually make most their money from teaching martial arts

62243840
I HATE THIS!!!

62243844
>>62243452
I keep warning /biz/ about this

most people lose everything not just once, but several times in life. It's normal. Learn to deal with massive disappointment. Don't let it ruin your day.

62243847
>>62243844
there must be a way to live without money!!!!

62243866
>>62243847
if you're in the US it's pretty easy to live without money. Not super comfy, but easy

62244008
^^^^^^^^^^AT LEAST I DON'T HAVE TO DEAL WITH INEQUALITY^^^^^^^^^^
HAHAHAHAHAHA

62244250
I really want to die!

62244257
>>62243844
:(

62244275
>>62243452
>povertism? No no
>pervertism - yes yes
Hang out in parks and creep on MILFs and tween girls.

62244277
>>62243452
Men were meant to build, women were meant to consume. I get no pleasure from buying things.

62244309
>>62244277
what do you mean?

62244383
>>62244275
:(

62244860
>>62244275
>Hang out in parks and creep on MILFs and tween girls.
Sitting on a park bench
Eyeing little girls with bad intent
Snot′s running down his nose
Greasy fingers, smearing shabby clothes
Hey, Aqualung

62248185
>>62243847
be black

62248230
>>62243479
Imagine believing this nonsense. I have a castle in France to sell you bro

item.title: WHAT IS THE MEANING OF THIS DUMP — 6 replies — 8.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHAT IS THE MEANING OF THIS DUMP

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-19 05:05 UTC

OP

WHAT IS THE MEANING OF THIS DUMP

Early replies (first 3)

62246402
>>62246395
just a dip tho

62246415
setPrice(700)

62246457
>>62246395
Chainlink was above $10, no other reason needed

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62246461
>>62246395
Drumpf being a nigger

62247643
>>62246395
>uncertainty of a bear market
>long liquidations

item.title: Why are we ok with the current housing situation — 7 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why are we ok with the current housing situation

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-19 04:13 UTC

OP

Hello to everyone, I am 28 y.o who cant understand how its possible to buy a flat/house in current economy. All of us need to live somewhere its obvious but why should we pay so much for the house that has been built in 70-80 and cost 4x lower than today. Logically item's quality goes down with the time even if you fixing a lot, its natural life cycle but in case of housing we all agree with the current prices and speculations of the big capitals (elites). Is it a problem to build new houses? No. The same issue was in history in the past especially after the 2 war. Government could afford to build enough houses, its so fucking crazy that in Bcn you can buy such house but its gonna cost you an arm and leg. So the problem of developing new houses is so artificial and big capital is playing with house prices while we cant even afford our BASIC needs. What shall we do with it? cooperate and demand

Early replies (first 3)

62242833
>>62242758
It's cheap in Nashville Tennessee which is where all the biggest companies are moving.

62242848
>>62242758
People adapt to eating shit and are fools with their money. People will get exorbitant high interest loans and adjusted mortgages they can't pay.

Nowadays a couple who separately make $50k annually will get together and co-sign on a $300k home that is really worth less $200k.

The elites know they can just do this freely and without any restraint but eventually this whole system will fully collapse. Unfortunately it will be a frogs in slowly boiling water type of thing.

62242862
>>62242758
real estate will 10x over the next 5 years, and there's nothing you can do about it, can't stop this train baby

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62242879
>>62242862
unironically this

Elites just don't give a shit anymore and are convinced the world will end soon which is why they're so relentlessly unchecked

62242887
>>62242758
>I am 28
You were supposed to live with your parents, where did your 10 years of income go?

62244610
>>62242887
i am 28 but I left my home country, thats why I lived 10 years in rent appartments.
Also take into account inflation and unexpected issues that we all have. Anyway people who lived 10 years with parents still cant afford to buy a flat/house in Spain for example. Just stupid plan of destroying affordable housing by elites makes me angry about it all. Sounds like being a squatter isnt bad idea

62248209
You cant stay put without family support

item.title: How much you need to retire in Weston, MA? — 60 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much you need to retire in Weston, MA?

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-05-19 02:21 UTC

OP

How much you need to retire in Weston, MA?

Early replies (first 3)

62246923
>>62246889
Looks mold prone, I'd suggest if you need to live in the middle of a town consider the midwest, some older towns you can find historic homes in rough condition but stucture is sound, you're gonna spend money regardless on what house you buy if it's 20 years old or worse.

62246973
I grew up in a town over, you will need a lot. A house like that is prob 2 mil or more.

62247007
>>62246973
is 2 million a lot there? Serious question. In a lot of HCOL areas in the US 2 million would be a very cheap house.

>>62246889
depends how old you are and how you plan on living. If it's an expensive town, housing is going to be your main obstacle and then how much you need depends on your strategy to secure it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62247293
>>62247284
he's been here a very long time, I guess he has corporate backing or something. I'm constantly surprised people fall for his shit but eventually there won't be any westerners for him to scam

62247299
>>62247293
He's been making fake gofundmes on /pol/ and upset that stupid Chudthebuilder got 200k, he spent 3 days kvetching that a white man got 200k.
Not that I give a shit I think all this e-streamer shit is pure cancer but you can tell if anyone literally anyone besides him makes money he throws fits, he also spams many many threads on both boards, he's some schizophrenic nigger who helped ruin this place.
He made over 6 figures scamming people here.

62247303
>>62247293
I shit you not he spends most of his days pretending to be christian to scam, then pretending to hate christcucks as he calls them so people feel more inclined to donate to him.
Guy might take the cake in terms of the most evil person I've had the displeasure of being around.

62247310
>>62247299
>>62247303
makes me wonder what other /biz/ characters he might be. Someone in /pmg/ once told me one of our oldest regulars in the thread was famous across the board for something I wouldn't believe.

62247313
>>62247310
Heh probably not I can tell you a poster here sued FTX, possibly talking about him.

62247320
>>62247310
It's possible though since those generals are highly botted infact /biz/ messed up the other day and it had 13 /SMG/ generals up at the same time and all had almost identical 1 line responses in the same spots.
It's sort of easy for bad actors to pick people out of those botted generals when they themselves setup the bots.

62247323
>>62247313
yeah that could be it. I can't imagine lebanon being too interested in metals unless he can scam or beg in the thread.

62247334
>>62247323
I'd presume it was him since he was rich for awhile, though hard times hit us all.

62247338
>>62247320
>/biz/ messed up the other day and it had 13 /SMG/ generals up at the same time and all had almost identical 1 line responses in the same spots.
kek
that's awesome!

we've been getting multiple redundant generals for a while now but not that many. I can't believe crypto is dying, but if /biz/ is any indication the best days of crypto are behind us.

62247346
>>62247338
Everything is gonna collapse the way we're going, btw crypto tends to crash on oil spikes.
in 2022 when gas spiked BTC had it's second big crash day comparing to the 2021 crash.
Oil is a good indicator on the real market the more it ratchets up everything will crash because we depend so heavily on oil.
And Trump is seriously retarded and I voted for him 3x.

62247369
>>62247224
>Will help you buy a house over a million dollars
>Monthly Mortgage payments of >$10,000
Good luck.

62247375
>>62247346
I didn't vote for him because I'm a boomer and I hated the guy in the 80's. But most boomers hated him back then and most of them love him now.

either way it's not like they gave us a great choice. Trump was the better option economically, but needs a babysitter to keep him from starting wars for israel apparently

62247381
>>62247375
He's clearly blackmailed, and has some weird delusion about leaving behind a legacy when he can't seem to do anything right, now it's the ballroom and love how he kicked off a middle eastern quagmire because nobody would give him a stupid peace prize.
It's clownworld for sure, I'm not quite a boomer but rapidly approaching 40.
I can't say my track record in voting is very good, I voted for Mccain my first vote.

62247386
>>62247369
I'm guessing it's a bunch of room mates going in together. Otherwise they'd go over the income limits for assistance. Either that or parents with high earnings that qualified either by disability or having 25 kids or something.

it happens though. In my town it's difficult to find a house less than one million, and we certainly have people on welfare that own houses.

62247409
>>62247369
also what anon and I were arguing about was ANY government assistance, not just welfare. Stuff like VA and FHA aren't means-tested but are still government assistance. This is something he can't understand. I make $15k per month and qualified for FHA assistance. Just because $15k per month is not enough to buy a house in my town. I get turned down for regular mortgages because my house payment is more than 1/3 of my income after taxes. FHA and VA can work around that though.

>>62247381
I liked McCain and Romney but I'm pretty old-school and a veteran. Trump's track record with veterans isn't great.

62247412
>>62247386
They also gave minorities loans if you recall few years back, which basically ushered in a bunch of niggers into the suburbs, and they don't ever pay rent like we do, if at all, you every so often see one get evicted here and typically ends in a shootout.
but you're right they each have 5-10 kids and each is on EBT so they get 12k ebt checks and mortgage assistance which like you said is likely calculated on the housing price not the person.

62247415
>>62247409
Just all the homo shit and ukraine shit and syria shit after that ruined Mccain for me and his butt body Graham ladybugs cracker.

62247423
>>62247412
exactly
in my area it's mostly mexicans, but same deal

those poor disadvantaged immigrants have a right to live right there with the rich people and we're going to help them. It's kinda funny to watch. You can see the barely-rich people thinking "well there goes the neighborhood!"

62247428
>>62247423
I got a 12.5 acre house I just inherited but there's a caretaker who's trying to take it so my realtor is dragging his feet, but she has no legal authority to do so she already stole everything of my aunts and her life insurance but I was able to secure the house with a TOD she can't steal.
But I also own a duplex in the city that's nearly paid off, I might end up moving to the house in Arkansas and renting this place out or selling it, though I hate to sell if things are about to hyperinflate.

62247450
>>62247428
Nice holdings! Yeah I'm holding onto real estate too, just because I don't expect the dollar to last. The decline is speeding up.

62247456
>>62247450
Im trying to sell the arkansas house since well it needs work but its on a scenic highway everyone is building shit shacks with 0.5 acres and asking 200k i just want 150k quick to walk away but nobody biting so I likely will just keep it fix it and ask 300k like everyone else.

Ideally I sell it and if a crash does happen I can buy a nicer house closer to my duplex, that's the plan anyway.

62247472
>>62247456
It's actually got a trailer with a foundation on it as well and metal tractor shed though it needs work the metals all old and lotta windows busted out in that, the trailers trashed my aunt ignored it but its got water and electrical hookups so you could knock the old trailer down plop in a new one and ask 500k for the property easily.
Which I might do.

62247727
>>62247117
It looks structurally sound though, who pays 10k for sanding and lacquering?

>>62247141
Retarded point desu

62247769
>>62247727
No i mean I worked in that town and there's much much worse and people want more.
That's decent for 99k could probably complain about the towns problems and lower it too.

62247858
>>62246889
Looks nice

62247895
>>62247010
>Sad in 08 it was probably 120k max.
LOLNO

Weston has been an "exclusive" extremely expensive area for at least the last forty years. Weston was easily half a million and up for even a shitty house in 1986, probably more like $750K.

Even in the 1980s they were having problems hiring wagies to work in their grocery stores and gas stations because there was ZERO "affordable housing" in Weston for the poors and their kids to live in -- you know, the people who would actually take a job at Subway instead of just telling mommy and daddy to increase their allowance because the price of premium gas for their high school daily-driver Porsche had gone up. The Weston city council even deliberated for a few months over rezoning some areas to allow mere apartments to be built but decided against it because they had to maintain their exclusivity.

62247901
>>62247032
Depends on whether you like the idea of living in Assholechusetts. I fucking hate that state. After I graduated college I swore never to go anywhere near it again. I've mellowed out, back then I would have cheered and danced if the USSR had nuked the shit out of Boston. As long as it happened on a weekend when the two friends I had who had stayed there were out in their suburban home, nothing of value would have been lost to the world.

62247928
>>62246889
Idk what it is about Boston real estate but it just never seems to come down no matter how inflated it actually gets. You’d think that wacky gook bitch mayor combined with kids opting out of the student loan goy trap would somehow work its way down into the property values but it just keeps going up forever

62247962
>>62246889
$20m

62247965
>>62247928
I have rentals in Boston. The wacky mayor has been great for rents by getting rid of the realtor fee. I pocketed the realtors fee and was able to raise rents 15% this year. Most I ever have in a single year before. Property values are never coming down again if you missed out on the financial crash

its a dysgenic thing, who knows

62244615

62244847
>>62244834

62244854
>>62244847
sure, there's one>>62244846

62244880
>>62244854

>one linkie brings up early fud spam

"these are the same thing"

62244901
>>62244826

Yeah it's bs
everthing is banned for spam/racism reasons
yet fud niggers can spam the board and it's all ok

62244922
>>62244615

62244931
It’s XRP baggies. They came from Reddit back in 2021 or so.

62244945
>>62244931
>It’s XRP baggies.
How do you know?
They seem not to care about anything except for LINK.

62244955
>>62244608
I'm... gonna....
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62244980
>>62244945
The verbose cripple is absolutely one of the resident Link fudders. In fact he's such a massive samefagger too that he's probably larping as multiple resident Link "villains".

62245610
Fuddies why not bump this thread?
Scared much?

62245791
>>62244587
Mental illness and faggotry.

62245798
>>62245610
fuck stinks

>>62245791
fuck stinks

62245832
>>62245791
kekbaggie.

62246039
>>62244587
All link fudders hold link. They were getting excited when it started pumping over $10 but since it crashed theyre all chimping out and lashing out again

62246104
that's great, people are posting about Chainlink more.

62246164
>>62246039
Sure, like the redstone monkey right?

62246168
>>62246039

Sorry about layer zero dude, it sucks to suck

62246257
>>62244980
I haven't seen the wall of text xerpie in a while

Sort of funny when he shows up because you can tell he tries to hold back, but inevitably ends up writing paragraph after paragraph of wordslop

62246264
>>62244587
see
>>62246166

62246271
I didnt contribute to any of the chainlink fud threads but I am certainly happy to see them. /biz/ is healing.

62246561
>>62246264
Yes I believe they are paid browns like the redstone monkey
perhaps a few do it out of mental illness but I doubt it
The nigerian "he na feel" slip was all you need to know

62246619
>>62244615
100% this.

62246964
>>62246561
>so he na feel like a ceo
>so he can* feel like a ceo

Its hilarious that in the unhinged schizophrenic mind of a linkcel, a simple typo can conjure up a vast tapestry of delusional plot lines about Jamaican fudders paid by the illuminati to fud their bags

62246989
>>62246964
>don't believe your lying eyes, goy

62247251
>>62246964
The thing is that fud is so boring now
just bland insults and whatnot
why don't you guys make an effort?
actually come up with something interesting?

62247391
>>62247251
the fud is boring? how about the shilling? how about link itself? who fucking cares about a $9 stable coin, bitch? show me the money. SHOW ME THE FUCKING MONEY

62247620
>>62247440
tl;dr = fuck every single stink

62247642
>>62247251
Price and chart. Thats literally it. Nothing else matters besides the price.
If the price was $1000, fud couldnt exist

62247656
>>62244587
>fuddie
they're just mocking you, they're not "fud'ing"

item.title: THE cuckolds of crypto — 86 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE cuckolds of crypto

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-05-18 15:14 UTC

OP

.

Early replies (first 3)

62238332
Fuck you bitch bastard

62238351
>>62238330
Insert "link" next tim pls, thanks!

62238706
>>62238330
so many Chainlink partnerships!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62242184
>>62242176
>since 2018
since 2021
blind nigga

62242191
simple as

62242193
>>62242184
He’s right you retarded nigger, if you bought Link in September 2017 you got outperformed by BTC. Congrats on being poor

62242204
>>62242193
What if you bought ETH at that same time?

Why doesnt this board have +10 ETH fud threads up all the time

62242205
>>62242193
he's a paid shill, don't even bother
he even fucked up and confused the fiat ATH with sats ATH
total imbecile

62242216
>>62242204
nobody is desperately trying yo convince me that ETH hasn't underperformed, get it now?

62242225
>>62242193
>if you bought Link in September 2017 you got outperformed by BTC
Wrong!
See:
>>62241119
LINK has outperformed BTC,
you can cope as much as you want fuddies
but it's just the facts

62242230
>>62242216
The narrative is that LINK has been worst investment you could have done back in 2017-2018 when ETH has performed 10x worse.

ETH doesnt even have Sergey dumping tokens so how is that possible?

62242249
>>62242225
LINK is sitting comfortably about -30% from its 2018 sats high, sorry but it's simple facts, it has underperformed BTC for almost 8 years now, each and every buy you did after that fact is in the red, and you clinging on to the ICO and Binance listing pump is disingenuous at best
sorry pal, can't argue with reality
>>62242230
you seem confused, that's YOUR cope narrative, that LINK was the only investment you could have made during 2017-18, while everyone else is simply pointing out to you that it's been a trash investment ever since
are you also retarded, like your gaslighting friend, or are you merely pretending?

62242250
>>62242193
>if you bought Link in September 2017 you got outperformed by BTC

LINK price (Sept 2017): $0.10
LINK price now: $9.69
LINK % gains: 9,590%

BTC price (Sept 2017): $4,000
BTC price now: $78,000
BTC % gains: 1,850%

LINK OUTPERFORMED BTC BY: 7,740%

62242252
>>62242249
No i just said that you could have invested in ETH for example

Why does that rile you up? Why arent you spamming catalog full of ai slopped Vitalik pics & ETH/BTC chart?

62242253
>>62242250
>the disingenuous shiller continues to play dumb and engage in shameless gaslighting
honey, the Sept 2017 ATH was $1.50, not $.010
the state of this board

62242258
>>62242252
cool, I'm just saying that LINK has been performing like garbage for almost 8 years, what the fuck is YOUR problem?

62242267
>>62242258
But LINK has literally outperformed BTC since 2018?

And pretty much the whole market

62242270
>>62242267
LINK is literally down from it's 2018 high in sats???
can you read a fucking chart bro?

62242275
>>62242253
>honey, the Sept 2017 ATH was $1.50
wtf are you smoking?

62242278
>>62242270
But it literally isnt? What kind of charts are you looking at

Anyway i literally couldnt care less, never selling

62242280
>>62242278
He's just making stuff up now it seems
lmao I broke the fuddie bot

62242285
keep embarrassing yourselves, that woll surely convince people to buy your heavy bags

62242297
>>62242285
No one here sold when it literally dumped to 0.00001 , what are you trying to achieve?

62242324
>>62242285
You don't need to buy Link. Actually I am totally happy currently with people not buying Link. I wish the price would crash even harder now that I am not a poorfag student anymore and have the money to buy more.
Chances are high I will still be very wealthy in a few years while you will stay a dumb useless shitskin anyway sorrounded by other shitskins, without any prospects in your pathetic life other than posting shit online for a few cents a day.

62242431
>>62242116
rattled plus whiter than you, moroccan transplant
still not posting portfolio huh? show us how you picked all the "winners" (lol) from 2017, go ahead

62242727
>>62238330
sirgay plz pump this shit already
i can't w8 any longer

62243376
>>62242431
His life is basically hell
He's a sandnigger who is obsessed with nigger dicks, and is such a gooner that it makes him suicidal
He also sold most of his link low (after buying at $30) to buy linksol and layerzero (and we know how those turned out LOL,) hence the obsessive rage over link and link holders

62244078
>>62243376
The lil sand nigger got btfo here so he's making like 10 new fudfag threads

62244381
>>62242431
>>62243376
Based Adem mind raping cuckold baggies.

He WON
Linkies LOST

62244443
I'm... KABOOOOOOMING

62244446
>>62244443
pic related is basically kryptonite for the cucks. They can never recover from this tweet

62244554
>>62239124
I am jewish and I want you to use all of your savings to buy link, yes

62245570
>duuuuuuuuude don't believe your own eyes

>duuuuuuuuuuude we're still doing better than insert horrific coin here

>duuuuuuuuude just go back to 2017 and buy


hilarious that the more the cult advocates argue, the more turned off their current and potential investors become

thereby the cycle perpetuates. the entire investment space sees these nasty angry incels shilling link, and passes

you may not ever make money on link again...but hey, at least you had that big run 6 years ago

THE Cuckolds of crypto

item.title: There are exactly 21.6 basis points sitting between you and the worst stock market crash in US history. — 29 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are exactly 21.6 basis points sitting between you and the worst stock market crash in US history.

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-05-19 00:13 UTC

OP

There are exactly 21.6 basis points sitting between you and the worst stock market crash in US history.

Early replies (first 3)

62243422
>>62243415
Good, i want sub1500 peso eth finally.

62243423
oh my science, is that my hecking yen carry trade

62243425
Nigga all my money is in xrp nip bonds dont mean shit to me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62243441
Oh thank God finally

62243443
>>62243415
Yeah no one is panicking here, it's bad for Japan, and the world. 3% is panic, 4% is basically death spiral, but this can easily accelerate. Importantly even if there's no direct or immediate impact on the rest of the world it'll set a tipping point for everywhere else

62243526
>be me
>scared to buy 2 months ago
>wasn't scared to buy the last 2 weeks
Anyway that's how I knew the crash was imminent, because I only feel confident buying at the worst times.

62243545
>>62243415
I don't get it.
What's so bad a gook bond paying 3% a year?

62243608
>>62243545
They're under so much debt at this point that even 3%, 4% basically is unsustainable

62243621
>>62243608
>>62243545
Oh basically so then Japan collapses under hyperinflation, the yen tanks, huge amount of global capital goes bust, huge hurt to their economy which hits everyone. But more importantly that'll instill discipline in the rest of the bond market, which will push loads of countries with shitty debt levels over the brink as well. Domino effect. Global fiat collapse

62243653
>>62243415
Nothing will happen. Just bought more unranium

62243661
>>62243621
Interesting. And what does this mean for my meme coins ?

62243662
>>62243415
I'm doing everything I can to tank america (selling u.s. bonds) and prop up Japan (bought a made in Japan Mazda, japanese knives for my kitchen, japanese stationary, japanese hentai)

62243664
>>62243526
Successfully lured by the jews

62243757
There are exactly 21.2 basis points sitting between you and the worst stock market crash in US history.

62243771
Hell yeah, inflation makes my assets go up, meaning I can borrow more later to buy depressed assets!
Odds on Bitcoin going back to $35k - $40k by October?

62243782
>>62243771
80%

62244081
>>62243526
>>62243621
the more fiat collapses, the higher stocks go. it's a melt-up crash in securities and a melt-down crash in fiat.
buy every dip because this is the last chopper out of nam

62244087
>>62243526
>letting emotions influence your trading strategy
lmao

62244174
>>62243653
Im in LEU not a pure uranium play but in the sector

62244891
>>62243415
they have been saying hat ever since it passed 1%
also nobody remembers 2022 when they said the same about us rates going beyond 2%
still waiting on the worse than 2008 crash

62244902
What do I do with this information
Get spooked?

62245089
Keep an ear out for what the BOJ decides to do in June and the market reaction to that. If you have investments in Japanese assets, be ready to potentially move them around. If not, then just don't care unless the Japanese economy crashes.

62245806
>>62244174
UUUU is better because it includes rare earths

62245845
>>62244891
There is some tipping point that no one exactly knows, but 4% is basically unsustainable with current debt. If you default then rates go skyhigh, if you print yen you get hyperinflation, economic collapse and the rates further spiral out of control.

62246504
It's not all going to crash until CDBCs are fully ready and they're not yet

62246783
>>62245845
for $500 anon what is yield curve control
it is the only realistic outcome of this mess and it is inflationary, not exactly hyper but it absolutely isnt mega bearish
nobody with their own money printer is going to default, they will print

everyone in the world has looked at greece in the wake of the gfc and has very clearly seen that adherence to austrian austerity bullshit to autistically balance some numbers on a balance leads to utter national ruination

62246807
>>62246783
you mean boj's gonna force japanese banks to buy longdated bonds at shit rates? when can we look forward to that? what happens to the mythical carry trade blowup?

62246835
>>62246807
the boj is just going to monopolize the entire bond market, nobody is going to care, all the doomers will pull their hair out
the carry trades impact on global asset valuations is greatly exaggerated, any twitter retard that thinks that japans bond printing has created the entire valuation of the ai tech complex is beyond retarded
just look at the relative numbers of valuation

62247702
>>62243661
Gongo?

62244025
>>62244013
link boom
so boom
bullish link
kaBOOOOOOM

62244248
>>62240967
>refute that the crypto market is fake and gay!

We know it is. Take one look at the top 10.
Why do you think crypto price action is up ass creek while stocks are at ATH?

62244357
>>62244013
>while /biz/cels are bagholding chainlink from a decade ago and they havent made any gains in years
speak for yourself you stupid nigger. I have 40k LINK staked since v0.1 and im up 600% ytd in my stock portfolio thanks to semis, memory and photonics.

you dumb pieces of shit are ALWAYS projecting your poorfag spirit onto others. i dont post alpha here cuz i refuse to give crumbs to LINK fudders kek

62244374
>>62244357
your shitcoin is down -85% since 2021. It was the most atrocious investment ever, nobody needs your shitty crumbs about LINK lmao

62244460
>>62244374
Good thing you devoted your entire life to thinking and posting about this all day every day.

62244909
>>62240993
yeah and from the same perspective xrp's chart looks better
should I sell my link and buy that trash instead hmm?

62244910
>>62244909
>yeah and from the same perspective xrp's chart looks better
Incorrect.
LINK has outperformed both BTC and XRP.

62244925
>>62244910
weird how I feel raped even though I'm up 3x still from my dca many years ago and in that same time if I had bought btc instead of link I'd be up ~15x and if I bought xrp I'd be up ~10x
so show me the crazy specific timeframe that link did better
was I supposed to buy ico and sell the top?

62244930
>>62244925
sorry xrp would have been 5x, btc would be like 12-15x

62244939
>>62244013
I'm making money on tech while also holding link. It's not really bag holding because I bought at 30 cent

62244943
>>62244925
>>62244930
LINK has outperformed XRP.
Cope all you want, it's just the facts.

62245194
>>62240980
Act like you belong here fagbag. Self checking is reddit

62245221
>>62244943
kek
full tard detected
>no wonder your in shitlink

62245265
>>62245221
WoW dud you are right!
Just market sold all my stinks!

62245492
>>62244357
>I have 40k LINK staked since v0.1

you're down 1.6 million

>i dont post alpha here

bro you can be honest... you have no alpha

62245509
>>62244943
>LINK has outperformed XRP.

not lately... xrp went 7x in the '25 run whereas link could barely double

it's funny watching the cult gaslight their newer members for not getting in early on the pyramid scheme. really smart community decision to shit all over your investors. then they're shocked there's no new buyers

62245517
>>62245492
You've been spending literal years of your life comparing other people's ROI like this.

62245520
>>62242019
OK link me to anyone with a portfolio anywhere on the entire internet with bnb that's 5 or 6 figures. You can't because they don't exist, this board is trash so what's your alternative, twitter where they openly run scams? Reddit where they're years behind everything (gme started here, reddit only picked it up at the very end)?

62245534
>>62245517
Its because they're comparing their own ROI to what they think they could have had. They all hold link. They're just too dumb to understand the complexity of the market and think its as simple as "someone smarter than me bought the better asset!", as if everyone here could have just bought BNB 6 years ago and been millionaires. When everyone DOES buy, like trumpcoin, it rugs immediately.

62245561
>>62245534
>They're just too dumb to understand the complexity of the market and think its as simple as "someone smarter than me bought the better asset!"
I think it's a lot dumber than that even, they're blaming the project for the market.
Basically pic related.

62245695
>>62244013
How many of the OGs actually held until now?

62245721
With picrel. Image from 3 days ago, but whatever.

62245775
fuck link chuddies

62245782
>>62244013
I'm still here taking a shit from my venice hotel room rn. Link will moon, you will see. It's just taking longer than expected. Which is a golden opportunity for those who missed the others.

62245800
>>62245517
>You've been spending literal years of your life comparing other people's ROI like this.

don't be so dramatic. it's an investment board. calculating gains/losses and opportunity cost is part of the discussion.


>>62245561
>I think it's a lot dumber than that even, they're blaming the project for the market.
>Basically pic related.

i think you have to tell yourself that lie in order to protect your ego. you have to tell yourself there's just no way an investor can't understand your genius play. they would have to be indian scammers to not see it

pretty juvenile thinking but i guess when you're down 5+ years straight and just missed the bull market, you have to come up with something

62245837
>>62245782
Literally nothing will pump this shitcoin, it will suffer the same fate as Kleros, an infinite permableed until a massive -50% wick destroys the spirit of remaining 2017 bagholders.
There are no net new big wallets, same baggies rotating the same bags, pretending staking yield makes up for a chart that looks like a stairway to hell.
Chainlink is one big permissioned, KYC'd, network, nobody with a crypto ethos wants to touch this trash with a 10-foot pole.
The biggest joke? LINK absorbed billions in TVL and still can't match LZ's volume.

62245840
>>62245721
>uncle rico bragging about gains from 7 years ago


bro we get it, you got some chicks' number in high school


the link cult is basically akin to boomers bragging about how they bought a house back in the day for $50k. they've conflated existing during a certain time period with having actual investment skill.

but that's the great thing about investing. even a blind squirrel finds a nut once in a while

62245849
Touch me

62246030
>>62244943
>>62245509
this
what the faggots don't realize is that xrp has had like 11 years for you to buy where you'd still be up and like 2 weeks in 2018 and last year to buy to be down
chainlink has had many more years for you to buy to be down and it's newer than xrp
thankfully I'm not down or I'd hate link (and myself) even more

62247125
>>62244939
>It's not really bag holding because I bought at 30 cent
you watched it do an inverse 10x, that qualifies

item.title: what do i do? — 1 replies — 4.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
what do i do?

Open thread • Replies: 1 • Updated: 2026-05-19 05:41 UTC

OP

before this, and as a side comment: i know there have been thousands of posts just like this one, but maybe, with some luck, a random comment could genuinely save my life

what do i do? im from Argentina, im 17 years old and just 1 month away from turning 18. honestly, i don’t know what to do. i have no money and no source of income. i didn’t enroll in university because i felt like all universities in Argentina were useless, just like the people i could end up meeting there. i thought about studying abroad, but it seemed like too much effort.

i have enough resources (PC, laptop, phones), but not enough money to bring several of the ideas i’ve had to life in order to generate something. and i have about a month left before my parents start pressuring me to get a job at burger king or some factory because “this house isn’t a shelter for lazy people"

what option do you think is viable for me? what can I invest my time and money into that would actually be worth it? or is there a way to find a path forward? what would you do if you were in my situation?

Early replies (first 3)

62248336
>>62248312
What ideas? Are you cute?
Sell socks on the train in order to pay for a claude/chatgpt/google subscription and then run some shitty online business or scam

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: WHEN WILL HE FUCKING STOP — 20 replies — 3.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHEN WILL HE FUCKING STOP

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-19 00:40 UTC

OP

I'M SO FUCKING SICK OF THIS BOUNCY BALL UP AND DOWN UP AND DOWN TRAMPOLINE MARKET MANIPULATION, FUCKING FUCK OFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF

Early replies (first 3)

62247452
Niggers

62247473
If you seriously think the market cares about what trump tweets you are cattle

62247476
>>62247448
The market is trying to price in him actually going to war, so if he says they aren't planning imminent war prices have to go down until the next escalation.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62247479
>>62247448
wow!

62247492
>>62247473
it does because this tweet created the last hour bounce back and ah pump

62247499
>>62247448
Its already bleeding back down, so much for the elon doge tweet!

62247512
Unironically
I wish I had gone all in with his bouncy ball.

62247515
>>62247499
It will be green tomorrow, people are in the euphoria stage.

62247519
>>62247473
Moo motherfucker.

62247527
>>62247519
that guy has TDS
only a moron would think trump cant manipulate the market

62247566
>>62247448
say his name.

62247585
>tfw sunk some reputation in my libtard family by supporting Trump originally
Should have been full on libtard anti-Trump. They were right about everything

62247613
>>62247448
Why? Its so easy. The guys who made pic related in march is probably retired by now.

62247629
>>62247492
>I care about microstructure
>No I don't make any money

62247650
>>62247448
Trump is full of shit. Just use this bounce to add to your short, you are shorting already right?

62247654
>>62247585
They were wrong about damn near everything and the part that was right (Israel having control of our war machine) was viewed as alt right nazi stuff back when they first opposed him.

62247669
When you guys are done having your little meltie about orange man bad the important thing to notice is that the taco tweets are no longer calming the markets down. We're starting to hit actual physical shortages and reserves are depleted. For the last two months the market was pricing in a fast resolution to the hormussy and now the overall market sentiment is shifting to the long term scenario.

62247695
>>62247448
TL:DR version for anyone sick of walls of text

President Trump announced a postponement of a planned military strike on Iran following diplomatic intervention from Gulf allies, while maintaining that a full-scale assault remains an option if negotiations fail. This statement is categorized as "bullshit" (in this context meaning theatrical) as it likely masks operational funding constraints and congressional pressure behind a public display of diplomatic leverage.

62247706
>>62247695
Well his hands are tied. If he starts bombing cities the world will call him a butcher and destroy any diplomatic support he hasn't already lost. If he meets Iranian demands he will forever be considered a total failure. He's attempting to save face but no one's buying it anymore.

62247755
>>62247706
There is a third option which I believe they might try. Trump could become "incapacitated" and allow JD Vance to take over to carry out the withdrawal or bombings, then trump resumes being president afterwards
